summary: First testing pass for Studio PM. Backend: 29/29 pytest tests passed covering auth (login/me/refresh/cookies), RBAC for clients/dashboard/gantt/leaves/tasks, projects CRUD + public share link flow, tasks CRUD with cascading subtask delete, events/leaves CRUD, and CSV exports for projects/tasks/users/events. Frontend: admin/manager/team/client login flows all work; role-based sidebar visibility is correct (team hides clients/sales/dashboard/financials, client only sees calendar+projects+profile); Projects page grid/list/gantt views switch, project cards open detail page with overview/tasks/calendar/team tabs and public toggle+copy share buttons; Tasks search/filter/kanban toggle/add modal create flow works end-to-end; Dashboard renders all 4 KPIs (Active projects, On track, Needs attention, Team size) and Gantt rows; Calendar shows seeded events (Kickoff, Northwind weekly sync, All-hands, Studio offsite) and leave indicators; Public project URL renders without auth and shows project name + tasks list.
action_items: Minor naming inconsistency vs review_request spec: Calendar prev/next buttons use data-testid 'calendar-prev-month' and 'calendar-next-month' instead of the 'calendar-prev-btn'/'calendar-next-btn' suggested in tests. Buttons exist and work; only the testid names differ. Optional rename for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1000 lines single-file; consider splitting routers per resource (projects, tasks, events) for maintainability before adding more features.

Seed runs on every startup with idempotency on entity name/email — fine for now, but tasks/events/leaves are seeded only when collections are completely empty. After manual deletion of all seeded tasks, restart no longer re-seeds. Acceptable for MVP.
Project members list contains user_ids but no membership cleanup hook when a user is deleted (no /users DELETE exists yet, so not an issue today).

Public CSV exports are user-gated but a 'client' role can still call /api/export/projects.csv (not gated to leadership/manager) and download all project rows including non-Acme ones. Consider role-gating CSV exports or filtering by visibility.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the requested flows (admin/team/client login, sidebar role gating, projects views+detail+tabs+public toggle, tasks search/filter/kanban/add modal, dashboard KPIs+gantt, calendar events+leaves, public share URL)

test_credentials: Using /app/memory/test_credentials.md: admin@studio.com/admin123, maya@studio.com/password123, arjun@studio.com/password123, client@acme.com/password123 — all seeded and verified.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Used backend's own startup seed. Test-created records were prefixed TEST_ and cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py runs against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and is reusable. Auth uses httpOnly cookies (SameSite=None,Secure) — pytest uses requests.Session() to auto-handle cookies. For UI tests, the public/projects/:token route requires a fresh browser context with NO cookies to truly test the unauth path; the seeded Acme project has public_enabled=true by default. Calendar prev/next testids are 'calendar-prev-month' and 'calendar-next-month' (not 'calendar-prev-btn'/'calendar-next-btn'). All other sidebar/page/tab testids follow the expected naming.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues found. The only deviation is a testid naming difference for Calendar nav buttons, which is cosmetic.
